title: Re-run DIY Heretic ablation on gemma3:4b with --batch-size 32 from t=0
description:
**Goal:** Validate the full Heretic DIY pipeline (HF model -> abliteration -> GGUF -> Ollama) end-to-end on `google/gemma-3-4b-it`, the smallest viable model. Use conservative batch flags from the start to avoid the known M4 Pro batch-128 stall.

**Why:** Phase 2 of the research plan. Phase 1 confirmed that *prebuilt* heretic models work, but we have not proven the *DIY* pipeline works on the M4 Pro. The first attempt on Qwen3-4B stalled at batch-128 for 30+ min (Hermes Session 2, 2026-06-04) -- RSS dropped 89 to 23 MB, tee log not flushing, process alive but silent.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Restart with `--batch-size 32 --max-batch-size 32` from t=0 (skip the 64 to 128 auto-detection)
- [ ] Use `unbuffer python -u heretic ... 2>&1 | tee -a results/qwen3-4b-heretic.log` to fix the line-buffered `tee` issue noted in the Session 2 stall
- [ ] Use `--print-responses --no-plot-residuals` (skip the plot, save time)
- [ ] If it finishes: convert to GGUF, import to Ollama, run A/B against stock `gemma3:4b`
- [ ] If it stalls again at batch-32: capture the failure mode + MPS stats + add to `docs/RESEARCH_PLAN.md` Known issues
- [ ] Reference bead ID in commit message

**Files / areas:**
- `results/<date>-gemma3-4b-heretic/` (model output dir)
- `docs/RESEARCH_PLAN.md` (update with results)
- Source model: `google/gemma-3-4b-it` on HuggingFace

**Depends on:** Heretic + kernel patches working (already validated, 2026-06-04). GGUF conversion needs `llama.cpp/convert_hf_to_gguf.py`.

**Assigned to:** Hermes (M4 Pro / Heretic execution side per the slack split agreement).

**Refs:**
- Slack thread: https://jleechanai.slack.com/archives/C09GRLXF9GR/p1780291876.887979
- Research plan: `docs/RESEARCH_PLAN.md` Phase 2 End-to-end local ablation
- Prior stalled attempt: msg 1780547741 (Session 2)

title: Long-context erotica A/B test on svjack/gpt-oss-20b-heretic (repro dual-model OOM)
description:
**Goal:** Re-run the Phase 1 erotica A/B test with a long-context prompt (target: 2k token prompt + 3k token response) on `svjack/gpt-oss-20b-heretic` vs stock `gpt-oss:20b`, using **two sequential requests** (one per model) to repro the original Phase 1 first-OK / second-failed OOM pattern.

**Why two sequential (not one giant request):** Matches the actual Phase 1 OOM signature (Hermes msg 1780622366 + 1780622376: first request to heretic succeeded, second failed; both models already loaded, MPS killed one under context growth). A single giant request would OOM both simultaneously and lose the A/B comparison.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Run on `svjack/gpt-oss-20b-heretic` and stock `gpt-oss:20b`, sequential (one finishes before the other starts)
- [ ] Capture full transcript + token counts to `results/2026-06-05_erotica-smoke/`
- [ ] Capture Ollama log + `ps aux | grep ollama` + MPS memory stats before/during/after
- [ ] Confirm or refute the dual-load MPS kill theory (bead jleechan-sj4)
- [ ] Update `jleechan-sj4` with confirmed/refuted root cause
- [ ] Update `docs/RESEARCH_PLAN.md` "Known issues" section with the workaround
- [ ] Reference bead ID in commit message

**Files / areas:**
- `results/2026-06-05_erotica-smoke/` (transcripts)
- `docs/RESEARCH_PLAN.md` (workaround doc)
- `jleechan-sj4` (parent bead — close it on resolution)

**Depends on:** Nothing — Ollama + both models already pulled.

**Assigned to:** Hermes (M4 Pro / Ollama execution side per the slack split agreement).

**Refs:**
- Slack thread: https://jleechanai.slack.com/archives/C09GRLXF9GR/p1780291876.887979
- Phase 1 baseline: commit 6992f02, `results/phase1-smoke/erotica_transcript.txt`
- Parent bead: jleechan-sj4

title: Make spicy_llm reproducible from committed harness files
description:
Finding: /Users/jleechan/projects_other/spicy_llm/README.md line 5 describes the repo as a reproducible harness, and README.md lines 23-31 list benchmark protocol docs, patch files, and scripts, but the tracked tree does not contain runnable harness scripts or committed patch files. The local heretic/ checkout is ignored by /Users/jleechan/projects_other/spicy_llm/.gitignore line 42, so even heretic/SOURCE.md is not available from a fresh clone.

Suggested fix: either add the minimal committed harness surface promised by the README (scripts, patch docs/files, benchmark protocol, source pin instructions) or revise the README to accurately describe the repo as documentation plus captured evidence until the harness exists.

Acceptance criteria:
- A fresh clone can follow committed instructions to reproduce the intended smoke or DIY flow, or the README no longer claims that level of reproducibility.
- Required upstream Heretic ref/patch information is tracked outside ignored heretic/.
- README repository layout matches files actually committed.

title: Fix results allowlist so only intended artifacts are trackable
description:
Finding: /Users/jleechan/projects_other/spicy_llm/.gitignore line 29 says results/ is ignored by default and only selected small artifacts are committed, but the current pattern only ignores top-level results/* and then reopens nested directories with !results/**/. Arbitrary nested files such as results/run/foo.json and results/run/raw-output.ndjson are trackable.

Suggested fix: change the default ignore to results/**, unignore directories only as needed, and explicitly unignore the approved artifact patterns. Verify with git check-ignore that arbitrary nested raw files remain ignored while REPORT.md, prompt files, selected transcripts, summary.json, NOTES.md, and config*.toml remain trackable.

Acceptance criteria:
- git check-ignore reports arbitrary nested result files are ignored.
- intended small artifacts still appear as trackable.
- comments in .gitignore match actual behavior.

title: Document MPS OOM / Ollama 500 root cause in dual-model A/B test
description:
**Symptom (reproducible but transient):**
- Running Ollama A/B test with both stock `gpt-oss:20b` (13 GB) and `svjack/gpt-oss-20b-heretic` (15 GB) loaded simultaneously on M4 Pro 51 GB unified memory
- First request to heretic model on a benign prompt returns Ollama 500 error
- Subsequent retry with shorter response succeeds — both models answer correctly

**Suspected root cause (unverified, from Hermes Session 3):**
- Both models loaded = 28 GB on 51 GB unified memory
- First request triggers context growth on both models simultaneously
- MPS allocation exceeds 8 GB cap, kills one model silently
- Ollama returns 500 instead of a clear OOM error

**Evidence in Slack thread C09GRLXF9GR:**
- msg 1780622366.838009: first crash observed
- msg 1780622376.641749: Hermes identified "13 GB RSS gpt-oss:20b" still alive, "heretic one likely got killed MPS"
- msg 1780622436.834819: retry succeeded, "Both handle the Fibonacci task correctly — no obvious capability loss"

**Status: RESOLVED via retry-with-shorter-context, but root cause not fully proven.**

**Next action (for this bead):**
- [ ] Reproduce on demand: pull both models, run long-context prompt on heretic, capture Ollama log + `ps` + MPS stats
- [ ] Try fix candidates: unload stock first, set `OLLAMA_NUM_PARALLEL=1`, reduce `num_ctx` to 2048
- [ ] Document the workaround in `docs/RESEARCH_PLAN.md` under Known issues
- [ ] Note: this is the M4 Pro "dual-model" trap, distinct from the M4 Pro "batch-128" stall documented in research plan

**Refs:**
- Slack thread: https://jleechanai.slack.com/archives/C09GRLXF9GR/p1780291876.887979
- Repo: jleechanorg/spicy_llm

title: Add sanitized or gate-friendly transcript artifact path
description:
Finding: git diff --check origin/main..HEAD fails because raw transcript artifacts include trailing whitespace/control-sequence artifacts, including /Users/jleechan/projects_other/spicy_llm/results/phase1-smoke/benign_transcript.txt line 13 and /Users/jleechan/projects_other/spicy_llm/results/phase1-smoke/erotica_transcript.txt line 42. This can block whitespace gates and makes review noisy.

Suggested fix: keep raw logs as raw artifacts if desired, but add a sanitized transcript/report path for committed evidence or configure gates to exclude raw transcript captures intentionally.

Acceptance criteria:
- git diff --check passes for normal committed source/docs, or raw transcripts are clearly exempted by project policy/tooling.
- raw-vs-sanitized artifact expectations are documented.
- evidence remains reproducible and human-verifiable.
